objective: Enable only the three approved read-only ORIS tools through the existing native OpenClaw plugin, verify natural-language use and latency telemetry in the native UI, and preserve automatic rollback to the tools-denied state. Do not add write actions in this task.

architecture_decision.plugin_role: typed read-only ORIS tools and sanitized lifecycle telemetry
architecture_decision.agent_harness_role: backend policy, routing, structured output and fallback
architecture_decision.oris_role: project authorization, task lifecycle, queue, leases, cancellation, retry, evidence and audit
architecture_decision.codex_role: real implementation, test, repair, commit and controlled push

read_only_readiness_requirements: validate the private install marker and pre-install backup without printing secret values
verify cold and runtime plugin inventory, exact three tools and exact three hooks
verify all approved tools remain in tools.deny before mutation
discover the effective tools.allow/profile/agent policy before deciding the minimal enablement change
verify scoped allowConversationAccess remains enabled only for the ORIS plugin
verify Gateway, public native UI, restricted routes and loopback-only internal services
verify telemetry path ownership, permissions and sanitized existing records
verify queue and completed product baseline are unchanged
verify no write tool and no active product task
controlled_enablement_acceptance: remove only the three approved names from the effective deny boundary and add explicit allow entries only if required by the discovered policy
keep submit, cancel and retry tools absent
restart only the existing OpenClaw Gateway when configuration validation passes
verify native browser natural-language use without ORIS-specific command syntax
verify no task submission and no queue mutation
verify model_call_ended, after_tool_call and agent_end telemetry without conversation or secret content
record response-latency baseline where the runtime exposes TTFT and total duration
automatically restore the tools-denied state on any failure
next_action: Create and run a GitHub-hosted read-only readiness script. Read its sanitized evidence from GitHub, then build a reversible read-only tool-enable and browser-smoke script. Do not enable tools manually and do not reinstall the plugin.
